assessor_1_raw:
## Independent Assessment 1: Long-Context Clinical LM vs. TF-IDF

Chronological split, patient-level assignment to prevent identity leakage, preprocessing on training only, equal per-label threshold optimization, document parity enforced. Design is methodologically rigorous.

Verdict: defense_wins
assessor_2_raw:
## Independent Assessment 2: Long-Context Clinical LM vs. TF-IDF

The patient-level split is a strong design choice. Equal tuning effort with matched conditions. Fiscal-year subgroup analysis for code update effects. MDC drift monitoring. No violations.

Verdict: defense_wins
assessor_3_raw:
## Independent Assessment 3: Long-Context Clinical LM vs. TF-IDF

The sliding-window mean-pool encoding correctly prevents context truncation from disadvantaging the TF-IDF baseline. Equal per-label threshold optimization is a key fairness control. Design is sound.

Verdict: defense_wins
adjudication_raw:
## Ensemble Synthesis (Run 1): eval_scenario_194

All three: defense_wins. Unanimous.

Verdict: defense_wins
